attacking_style: Wins from sustained power in final 500m on uphill or rising finishes (8.3% ramps tolerated), not explosive acceleration. Excels when pure sprinters are dropped on lumpy/climbing circuits.
team_tactics: Trek uses mountain-pace domestique work to shed rival sprinters before uphill finishes. Lead-out train executed when climbs preselect group, but lacks cohesion on technical flat sprints.
terrain_preference: Rising/uphill finishes with significant elevation (3100m+ stages survived), lumpy circuits that shed pure sprinters. Exposed on technical flat sprints and early crosswind splits in cobbled classics.
vulnerability: Early positioning failures in classics (caught behind crosswind splits, crashes before key sectors). Physical contact disrupts rhythm. Lacks explosive acceleration to beat top sprinters with superior lead-outs on flat finishes.
key_patterns: Won 6+ uphill/climbing sprints across TDF/Vuelta/Paris-Nice, unique durability profile for sprinter category
Recovers from being dropped mid-stage to contest finish, documented 3+ times across Giro/TDF
Trek specifically rides climbing tempo to drop rival sprinters, strategy repeated 2022 Vuelta and 2023 TDF
Multiple second-place finishes when positioned well but lacking final acceleration against pure sprinters with leads
Targets green jersey via breakaway intermediate sprints on mountain stages, not traditional sprinter approach
Catastrophic classics results when caught early: 2022 Roubaix crash, 2023 Roubaix capitulation, crosswind vulnerability
form_trajectory: positive: Won 3 TDF stages in 2023 including photo finish, survived unprecedented climbing for sprinter, climbing TT performance exceeded expectations significantly
